entry.title: Natively ABAP Cloud (Clean Core) Add-ons in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition
entry.content:
This blog highlights the new features introduced in the SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition, as part of the 2408.4 release in December 2024.

Add-ons in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition.

Customers implementing or operating SAP S/4HANA Cloud, Public Edition, in a three-system landscape now have the capability to deploy natively developed and seamlessly deliver clean-core ABAP Cloud partner add-ons within the stack. Yes, as you read, the public cloud now offers the ability for partners to create scalable add-ons, enhancing or extending functional or industry aspects within the public cloud. Best of all, these add-ons are developed following SAP’s ABAP Cloud development methodology applied to the public cloud's principle of extensibility, ensuring they are life-cycle compliant and maintain a clean core. This means they are upgrade-safe.

Until December 2024, packaged ABAP Cloud add-ons were only available for SAP S/4HANA On-Premise and SAP S/4HANA Cloud, Private Edition. Now, they are natively available as clean-core solutions for SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud! The integration of SAP Store publishing, order management, and fulfillment with automated deployment to customers' Public Edition systems makes ABAP Cloud add-ons even more advantageous compared to traditional ABAP Cloud add-ons. We believe this new capability will allow partners to offer our customers ample possibilities to enhance functional and industry-specific areas. Traditionally, SAP has relied on partner expertise to introduce new components or address specific complex processes. This innovation enables partners to further complement SAP solutions with their specialized offerings.

These add-ons are specifically designed for the public cloud, utilizing tools that are natively aligned with public cloud development. Consequently, the add-ons are built to be life-cycle compliant, eliminating the need for remediation after upgrades since they are created using SAP’s extensibility tools for the public cloud.

Even with these state-of-the-art extensibility tools, SAP can certify the add-ons to ensure they meet all the requirements of a certified add-on. This includes, among other criteria: the add-on must run on-stack on SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition, uninstallation must be supported, the implementation must adhere to the development processes described in the help pages for SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition, all objects must be located in a registered namespace, and the add-on must successfully pass a security code scan using the provided code scanning tool of ABAP Development Tools, among other.

For partners interested in developing scalable add-ons, SAP offers the central SAP Business Technology Platform service, Landscape Portal, which orchestrates Add-on Assembly Kit as a Service (AAKaaS), Continuous Integration/Continuous Delivery (CI/CD), and many other frameworks. With the Landscape Portal, partners can now build, deliver, and maintain their solutions as add-ons, while customers can seamlessly consume these add-on-based partner solutions. For more details, please refer to the following blog by my colleague, Oren Shatil: Extensibility - General Availability of Scalable Solution for SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition

As detailed in Oren’s blog, SAP is now offering partners a new Test, Demo, and Development (TDD) system that consists of a three-system landscape: Dev, Test & Build, and Showcase. This setup facilitates the development of add-ons in conjunction with an enhanced tool of the " Partner Landscape Portal." More details can be found in Oren's blog.

Add-on process for SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition: from creation in the partner landscape to deployment in the customer landscape.

What does this mean for customers?
SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition customers can benefit from partner add-ons, gaining additional enhancements or extensions tailored to functional or industry-specific solutions provided by specialized partners.

Customers will be able to browse and purchase these add-ons from the SAP Store. With automated deployment, these solutions can be easily made available in the respective customer systems.

Scalable Scenario for Add-Ons: Customer and Partner Process Using SAP Store.

It is recommended to initially deploy such add-on products in the development system of the customer's three-system landscape. This approach allows the product to be tested and integrated into the customer's environment. Once satisfied with the outcome, the product can be installed across all systems in the customer's three-system landscape using the Deploy Product app included in the Customer Landscape Portal.

Customers will be able to purchase and deploy add-ons directly from the SAP Store.

We believe these new capabilities will help our customers gain more specialized functionality derived from the extensive experience of many partners in the SAP ecosystem. Please note that this functionality has just been introduced, so we expect the number of available add-ons to increase over time.

If you would like to share your experiences with other SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition customers, feel free to join our online user group. For more information, click here.

I would like to thank my colleagues, Arturo Montes de Oca, Helmut Hofmann, Khaled Musilhy, Winfried Schmitt, Carsten Lessmann, Oren Shatil and Alexey Romanov, for their review and feedback on this blog.

Your insights and opinions are valuable, so please don't hesitate to contribute by commenting on this blog.

More Information on SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition:

    • SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition, release info here
    • Product videos on our SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition and SAP S/4HANA YouTube playlist
    • SAP S/4HANA PSCC Digital Enablement Wheel here
    • Early Release Webinar Series here
    • Inside SAP S/4HANA Cloud Podcast here
    • openSAP Microlearnings for SAP S/4HANA here
    • Best practices for SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition, here
    • SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition, Community: here
    • Feature Scope Description here
    • What’s New here
    • Help Portal Product Page here
    • SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition trial, test, demo, and development (SAP partner access)
    • SAP S/4HANA Cloud ABAP environment

Follow us via @SAP and #S4HANA or follow @Marco_Valencia

entry.title: SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition – Security Concepts
entry.content:
SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition security measures

SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition comes with the following security measures:

24/7 monitoring and threat detection: Data centers are continuously monitored by security operations teams, utilizing cutting-edge tools to detect and respond to potential threats in real time.

Compliance with global standards: Data centers comply with international standards and certifications, including ISO/IEC 27001, SOC 2, and GDPR, to help ensure that data is handled with the utmost care and in accordance with legal requirements.

Encryption and data protection: Robust encryption protocols are implemented for data at rest and in transit, safeguarding information from unauthorized access.

Access controls and audits: Access controls help ensure that only authorized personnel have access to critical systems and data. Additionally, regular audits are conducted for compliance with security policies and procedures.

Primary source of publicly available content can be found here in SAP Trustcenter https://www.sap.com/about/trust-center/security.html

Or in this blog-post and links collection to valuable security content: https://community.sap.com/t5/enterprise-resource-planning-blogs-by-sap/everything-you-always-wanted-to-know-about-s-4hana-security-but-didn-t-know/ba-p/13573003

Datacenter Security

Source for information can be found in SAP Trustcenter: https://www.sap.com/about/trust-center/security.html

Attestations

The management systems are used across all SAP Cloud Secure services, execution of independent certification, and audit depending on service and organizational unit respectively.
Details are available at www.sap.com/about/trust-center/certification-compliance.html.

The following attestations are available to the S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition Service:

 

Q/A Section:

  1. Q: Which Datacenter Tier level is SAP offering?

A: The “DC Tier Level” rating can officially only be given through the “Uptime Institute”. Only very few of our DCs are certified by the Uptime Institute and are allowed to carry an official Tier Level. Because of this, we usually don’t use the word DC Tier, but instead something like “similar to Tier III“, this is also assessed as part of internal audits. Additional Info: Four Tiers are defined by the Uptime Institute standard:

Tier I - BASIC CAPACITY and must include a UPS (uninterruptible power source)

Tier II - REDUNDANT CAPACITY and adds redundant power and cooling

Tier III - CONCURRENTLY MAINTAINABLE and ensures that ANY component can be taken out of service without affecting production

Tier IV - FAULT TOLERANT allowing any production capacity to be insulated from ANY type of failure.

 

  1. Q: How do you ensure the data is not co-mingled between the customers when we use multi-tenancy?

A: On DB level, each customer is using a single database container completely separated from other customers, but in the same database. On top of that, the customer communication is separated via security groups taking care of strict separation per customer.

On Application server level, each customer is getting his own VMs and communication is as well strictly separated per security groups.

  1. Q: Is it possible to bring my own key/allowed for customers to bring their own key?

A: Yes, with Data Custodian that is possible.

  1. Q: Can we limit the access to the S/4HANA Public Cloud or Private Cloud to a specific IP address range (customer network)? Can we also limit the access of the APIs to specific IP range?

A: Our Identity Authentication services offer conditional authentication, of which IP range limitation is one possibility. Here’s the respective documentation: https://help.sap.com/docs/identity-authentication/identity-authentication/configure-conditional-authentication-for-application?locale=en-US

This is not applicable to APIs:

IP allowlisting is not supported for API access, as IP allowlisting is not a practical approach for public SaaS solutions as outbound IP addresses of source systems often change, e.g. in case of failover scenarios, or are shared across-customers on Hyperscalers.

From S/4HANA side, we recommend leveraging strong authentication mechanisms like mTLS to mitigate the risk of unauthorized APIP access.

 

  1. Q: Does SAP (or a third party) perform SAST/DAST scans (Static/Dynamic Application Security Scan) before delivering code? What tools are used? Are there any documentations we can send to the customer? How often are the scans performed?

A: Yes, SAP is performing Application Security Testing, both Static and Dynamic, as part of our Secure Development Lifecycle. For DAST and the main programming language ABAP, we’re utilizing our own solution CVA, whereas for other programming languages and DAST we’re using 3rd party tools, tailored to our own requirements. You can find out more about our secure development lifecycle in this document: https://www.sap.com/about/trust-center/search-finder.html?sort=latest_desc&search=software+development&pdf-asset=a248a699-627c-0010-82c7-eda71af511fa&source=social-atw-linkedin&page=1

 

  1. Q: Do we support mixed authentication (if the user doesn’t exist on Azure AD, he/she still can authenticate with Username/Password)

A: No. No fallbacks. Then simply the logon attempt will fail – by intend.

Anyway: It would not help to allow the user to “enter” the system since he’ll lack all authorizations and cannot do anything in the system.

 

  1. Q: Is the customer data encrypted?

A: My Trust Center” there is a document dedicated to SAP S/4HANA encryption:

https://support.sap.com/en/my-support/trust-center/tools-information.html?anchorId=fb2ab083fdbbcd2857bec883585ed034

Plus, the encryption is also mentioned in our help pages:

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/55a7cb346519450cb9e6d21c1ecd6ec1/2a2fc63ca8074738abb83a9a88cabec5.html?locale=en-US

Data in transit as well as data at rest is encrypted.

 

  1. Q: Please describe what Penetration Testing is carried out.

A: Penetration testing is a crucial security practice we perform to identify and address vulnerabilities in our applications and cloud infrastructure. We conduct various types of testing, including application, infrastructure, internal, and external penetration tests, as well as red team exercises, to simulate real-world attacks and improve our security posture. Regular testing, combined with continuous monitoring and remediation efforts, ensures the ongoing protection of our services and our customers' data.

 

  1. Q: Please describe your disaster recovery process. How is this tested?

A: Please see sep. detailed content document as well as ttps://support.sap.com/content/dam/SAAP/S4H_910%20Backup%20and%20Disaster%20Recovery%20for%20S4HANA%20Cloud.pdf

Tested annually and ISO22301 certified.

 

  1. Q: How do you prevent unauthorized access (hacking) and what processes you follow if this occurs?

A: To prevent unauthorized access, we employ a multi-layered security approach that includes robust authentication mechanisms, such as multi-factor authentication (MFA) and single sign-on (SSO), to ensure only authorized users can access our systems. We also implement strict access controls, network segmentation, encryption for data at rest and in transit, and regular security updates and patching to minimize vulnerabilities. Continuous monitoring using advanced threat detection tools helps us identify and respond to suspicious activities.

In the event of a security breach, we follow a well-defined incident response process that begins with immediate containment of the threat to prevent further damage. Our security team quickly analyzes the breach to determine its origin and impact, while simultaneously initiating communication protocols to inform affected stakeholders. After the threat is neutralized, we conduct a thorough investigation to identify the root cause, apply necessary remediation measures, and document lessons learned to strengthen our defenses and prevent future incidents.

The customer network is isolated from the SAP company network. Our Identity Authentication services offers conditional authentication to customer systems.

 

  1. Q: Can I limit network access to my instance,...? would it be possible to add blocking to the customer URLs for any IP other than theirs?

A: On S/4 Public Cloud, IP allowlisting is restricted to the SAP Identity Authentication Service (IAS), which is used to authenticate all customer business users. This leaves the gap for API traffic, though. As API traffic is sent directly to the S/4 Public Cloud system. IP allows listing directly on the S/4 Public Cloud is not possible.

entry.title: Creating SAP S/4HANA Embedded Analytics in Public Cloud with Developer Extensibility
entry.content:
Target audience of this blog: people like me. Caucasian, male, arrogant … just kidding. I mean of course experienced in data modeling (with SAP BW, for instance), experienced in creating embedded analytics in on-prem/ private cloud S/4HANA, but relatively new to Public Cloud S/4 (yes, I know, the full name is “SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition”; geez, get a life!). Let’s face it, we all are relatively new to Public Cloud S/4. And the topic of this blog is creating SAP S/4HANA embedded analytics in public cloud with developer extensibility.

So first of all, what is “developer extensibility”? When mother duck SAP conceived various versions of her new ERP system S/4HANA, she immediately became very protective of her ducklings, especially of the in her view prettiest one: public cloud. Noone was allowed to touch this duckling. Access to the database-side was – and still is – out of the question. Custom-specific modifications via the application-side were limited to two ways: “key user extensibility” or “in-app extensibility” and “side-by-side extensibility”. Key user extensibility is the use of certain key user tools like “Custom Fields and Logic”, “Custom CDS Views” and “Custom Analytical Queries”. Side-by-side extensibility is exporting data via an API to another system, usually the SAP Business Technology Platform, doing something with this data in this system, and then bringing the result back to S/4 also via an API.

With the on-prem/ private cloud duckling, mother SAP is less protective. Developers are allowed to connect to the applications-side with Eclipse and create objects in the development client. For analytics, the most useful objects are CDS-views: basic views, data-integration views, cube-views, query-views. For details, read my book [https://link.springer.com/book/10.1007/978-1-4842-7017-2]. To assess the developments options for a certain type of extensibility I apply two criteria: (1) which objects can be used as “starting points” for developments, and (2) which “operations” are possible? For starting points, one can think of SAP standard CDS-views, either released or not released, tables and database views. A full set of operations consists of left outer join on demand (ASSOCIATION), normal left outer join (LEFT OUTER JOIN), inner join (INNER JOIN), aggregation (GROUP BY), filtering in query (@Consumption.filter), intermediate filtering (WHERE) and union (UNION ALL). And the good news is: all starting points and the complete toolbox of operations are available for on-prem/ private cloud development in Eclipse. This led me to conclude that all transformation ‘tricks’ I managed to master in SAP BW over time can also be developed as “virtual data flows” in CDS-view stacks resulting in embedded analytics.

This is far from true for the key users extensibility apps “Custom CDS Views” and “Custom Analytical Queries”. Only starting point is standard CDS-views released for key user apps, and the only operations allowed are ASSOCIATIONs to connect master data to one set of transaction data in “Custom CDS Views” and analytics annotations, e.g. for filtering, in “Custom Analytical Queries”. That’s it. Who stole my toolbox? The union-operator is especially dearly missed, as this is the only way tot properly integrate two differently structured sets of transaction data.

And now the real-life business requirement. Customer recently went from SAP ECC to Public Cloud S/4 and is seeking an alternative for an ABAP program in ECC that did exactly this: integrating two differently structured sets of transaction data, i.e. financial documents and billing documents. Applying the key user apps starting from CDS-views released for key user apps I came a long way by displaying the required information in two separate queries, but without union-operator it is not possible to properly display Amount from financial documents together with Net Value from billing documents in the same query. We need to try something different.

Back to the original question: what is “developer extensibility” in public cloud context? I believe it is the result of pressure by the SAP developer community to allow more flexibility in the public cloud offering (otherwise no one is buying it). This first resulted in a shady project named “SteamPunk”, evoking an atmosphere of drug abuse in a squat, and later on the option to connect Eclipse to “client 80” of a public cloud S/4 system to carry out “cloud development” on objects “released for cloud development”. Cool! However, the word was that development options were quite limited compared with “non-cloud development”. But let’s find out for ourselves by trying to solve the remaining embedded analytics challenge.

How does “developer extensibility” work technically? Public Cloud S/4 has a system landscape as shown below (picture by courtesy of myBrand).

 

First you need to find out the url of client 80 of the Dev-system and request a user with proper authorizations. The Dev-system will have two clients: client 100 (Dev-Cust) and client 80 (Dev-Dev). The client is shown in the top bar in the browser.

For key user extensibility you need to work in client 100 via Fiori, for developer extensibility in client 80 via Eclipse. Eclipse can be connected to both clients, but only the connection to client 80 can be used to develop objects. In Eclipse, instead of “File” > “New” > “ABAP Project” you now need to go “File” > “New” > “ABAP Cloud Project” and enter the system-part of the url.

Logon via SSO in browser:

Now you have two Eclipse projects to work in: client 80 for development and client 100 for data preview and unit testing. As CDS-views are cross-client objects, all views created in one client are immediately available in the other client after refresh.

And how was my first experience with public cloud developer extensibility? As a notoriously grumpy old man, I am not easily made enthusiastic about something, but for this I would like to make an exception. Just look at the last column in the table below. I found my toolbox back!

What I ended up doing is create a union-view and a cube-view with developer extensibility in client 80 via Eclipse, and configure a query-view on top with key user app “Custom Analytical Queries” in client 100 via Fiori. I used the same methods as in on-prem development, and found almost no limitations. Below, ABAP CDS-code of the union-view is shown, which is a pretty neat piece of work, if you don’t mind me saying.

To name one limitation: casting to a data element has the advantage that not only the field-format is inherited, but also metadata like description. This, however, led to an error message only for public cloud.

Workaround is to cast to a field format like this in the union-view …

… and provide a label using an analytics annotation in the cube-view:

The real limitation of public cloud developer extensibility compared with on-prem development is not in the toolbox, but in the starting points. I was lucky that required CDS-views were released for cloud development, but this is not always the case. In fact, even less CDS-views are released for cloud development than for key user apps.

Good to mention: releasing CDS-views after development is also something you need to do as a developer. You need to release for cloud development if the next step is also in Eclipse and for key user apps if the next step is in Fiori. Below is shown how the cube-view is released for key user apps to configure a query-view on top (left) and the union-view is released for cloud development as well in order to build a cube-view on top (right).

       

To conclude: this is good stuff! Oh, and let me proudly show Amount from finance and Net Value from billing in the same query. I know, it doesn’t look that spectacular. But it is pretty smart.

entry.title: Bringing Structure and Guidance to Requirement and Approval Statuses in SAP Cloud ALM
entry.content:
SAP Cloud ALM continues to evolve, and the latest enhancement to the Requirements application introduces an important shift in how requirement statuses and approval states interact. Previously, users could freely update both approval and requirement statuses without restrictions, which offered flexibility but lacked process guidance. With the new capabilities, SAP Cloud ALM introduces structure, ensures alignment between approval and requirement statuses, and improves governance by controlling when requirement descriptions can be edited.

What Has Changed?

Conditional Status Selections Based on Approval State

In the past, any requirement status could be selected regardless of the approval state. This unrestricted flexibility is now replaced by a more guided approach, where available requirement statuses are determined by the requirement’s approval state. This ensures a clearer lifecycle and better control.

 

 

Approval and Requirement Status Mapping

To clearly understand how approval and requirement statuses now interact, refer to the table below:

 
Approval State

Requirement Status

No Approval Required

“In approval” no longer visible in dropdown

Selectable values are:
• In Refinement
• In Planning
• In Realization
• In Testing
• Successfully Tested
• Blocked
• Confirmed
• Not Planned

This state is best used for requirements that do not need a formal approval workflow.

 

Ready for Approval

The system sets the status to "In Approval".
No other status than “In approval” can be set until requirement is either:
• Approved
• Rejected
• Reverted to “No Approval Required”

This enforces discipline during the approval process and locks the requirement in a pending state.

Approved

The system sets the status to "In Planning" and allows you to change to:
• In Realization
• In Testing
• Successfully Tested
• Blocked
• Confirmed
• Not Planned

Once a requirement is approved, it follows a logical progression through implementation phases.

 

Rejected

The system sets the status to "Not Planned"

The requirement is effectively paused and marked as not moving forward.

 

From “Ready for Approval” to Not Assigned

(1) Requirement was in status “In Approval”
(2) Approval state changed (e.g., to “No Approval Required”)
(3) System sets status to "In Refinement"

 

 
 

Trail and Reset Capabilities

  • All changes to statuses and approval states are logged with timestamp and user information in the Requirement History.

     

  • Project Administrators can reset approved or rejected requirements to "Not Assigned", and the system will reset the status to "In Refinement". This gives flexibility when re-evaluation is needed without losing historical traceability.

 

Locking of Description Fields

Another major improvement is the locking of requirement descriptions in certain states to ensure clarity and integrity of the content:

  • Descriptions are locked when:

    • Approval State = No Approval Required or Rejected

    • Requirement Status = In Approval, Successfully Tested, or Confirmed

This prevents changes to critical requirements once they’ve reached a significant phase or decision point in the workflow.

 

Guidance Messages for Users

To support users, the system now provides real-time messages and hints when attempting status or approval changes. These messages:

  • Indicate the next possible or required action.

  • Explain why a status is locked or unavailable.

  • Help prevent invalid transitions.

 

 

New status "In Planning" in Requirements

The "In Planning" status has been introduced to address a common scenario in agile and hybrid project execution: teams don’t typically start working on a requirement immediately after it is approved.

Why This Status Was Needed

  • Previously, once a requirement was approved, it moved directly to statuses like "In Realization".

  • However, this caused confusion—since no work may have started yet, setting a requirement to “In Realization” prematurely was misleading.

  • The option “Not Planned” couldn’t be used either, as it is mapped to “Not Relevant” and implies the requirement is no longer considered for implementation.

Purpose of "In Planning"

To solve this, the "In Planning" status was introduced:

  • It acts as a holding state for all approved requirements.

  • It becomes the default status once a requirement is approved.

  • It allows teams to filter and review all approved requirements during planning sessions, such as sprint planning or wave definition.

  • From here, requirements can be prioritized and assigned to sprints for execution.

Only when the team actively begins working on the requirement does the status move forward to "In Realization".

Typical Activities in status "In Planning".

  • Assign effort estimates and responsible teams.

  • Link the requirement to a sprint or release.

  • Break down into user stories or implementation tasks.

  • Review dependencies and prepare for realization.

This structured intermediate step adds clarity and avoids confusion in status tracking, particularly in complex or large-scale projects.

 

Conclusion :

These enhancements bring much-needed structure and governance to requirements management in SAP Cloud ALM. By aligning requirement status transitions with approval states and locking critical information at the right times, the system now supports a more disciplined and auditable requirements lifecycle.

Whether you're refining your backlog or managing complex implementation phases, these updates helps ensure that your team follows a clear and consistent process from ideation to confirmation.

More Improvements are planned in the direction of bringing consistency, traceability, and clarity in requirement management. Stay tuned for more updates and guidance.

entry.title: Part 2: Local Handler Class, Draft Functionality, and Attachment Handling
entry.content:
Local Handler Class

In an unmanaged RAP scenario, the local handler class is where you write the ABAP code to implement the behavior defined in the behavior definition. This includes implementing the standard CRUD (Create, Read, Update, Delete) operations and any custom actions.

  • Role of the Local Handler Class:

    • It acts as the "brain" of your RAP business object, containing the code that interacts with the database and enforces business rules.
    • It processes requests from the service layer and performs the necessary actions.
    • It handles data manipulation, validation, and authorization.

    • Keys in Local Handler Class:

      • Keys are crucial within the local handler class because they are used to identify specific instances of the business object.
      • For example, when updating a customer, the customer_id key is used to locate the correct customer record in the database.
      • When creating a new entity (like an attachment), the handler might need to generate a new unique key.
      • Key fields are often used in WHERE clauses in database operations to ensure that the correct data is being accessed or modified.

      • Entities:

        • Within the local handler class, the term "entities" refers to the data being processed—typically, a table of instances.
        • When creating new customers, the "entities" parameter would hold the data for the new customer records.
        • When updating customers, "entities" would contain the modified data for the customers being updated.
        • When deleting, "entities" would identify which customer instances to delete.

        • CRUD Operation Examples:

          • Create:

            • This method handles the creation of new customer records.
            • It iterates through the entities to be created.
            • It generates a new customer_id (if it's not provided).
            • It inserts the new customer data into the zztmfcustomer database table.
            • It handles error situations (e.g., if the database insertion fails).
            • It returns the created customer IDs with %cid (client ID) to the framework.

            • Read:

              • This method retrieves customer data based on the provided keys.
              • It iterates through the keys (which contain the customer_id).
              • It selects the customer data from the zztmfcustomer table using the customer_id in the WHERE clause.
              • It appends the retrieved data to the result.
              • It handles the case where a customer with the given ID is not found.

              • Update:

                • This method updates existing customer records.
                • It iterates through the entities containing the updated data.
                • It uses the MODIFY statement to update the corresponding record in the zztmfcustomer table, using the customer_id in the WHERE clause to identify the record.
                • It handles potential errors during the update.

                • Delete:

                  • This method deletes customer records.
                  • It iterates through the keys (containing the customer_id of the records to be deleted).
                  • It deletes the records from the zztmfcustomer table using the customer_id in the WHERE clause.
                  • It includes error handling.
    METHODS create FOR MODIFY IMPORTING entities FOR CREATE Customer.
    METHODS modify FOR MODIFY IMPORTING entities FOR UPDATE Customer.
    METHODS delete FOR MODIFY IMPORTING keys FOR DELETE Customer.
    METHODS read   FOR READ   IMPORTING keys FOR READ Customer RESULT result.​

ASSOCIATION :

The Associated Child entities should be created only from a Parent Entity, in the above they are specified as Root\_Associated Entity , however the other operations of the class can be handled directly by declaring a method.

METHODS create_ContactMedium   FOR MODIFY IMPORTING entities FOR CREATE Customer\_ContactMedium.
    METHODS create_CreditProfile   FOR MODIFY IMPORTING entities FOR CREATE Customer\_CreditProfile.
    METHODS create_EventInput      FOR MODIFY IMPORTING entities FOR CREATE Customer\_EventInput.
    METHODS create_EventSubscription FOR MODIFY IMPORTING entities FOR CREATE Customer\_EventSubscription.
    METHODS create_Characteristic  FOR MODIFY IMPORTING entities FOR CREATE Customer\_Characteristic.
    METHODS create_PaymentMethod   FOR MODIFY IMPORTING entities FOR CREATE Customer\_PaymentMethod.
    METHODS create_RelatedParty    FOR MODIFY IMPORTING entities FOR CREATE Customer\_RelatedParty.
    METHODS create_TimePeriod      FOR MODIFY IMPORTING entities FOR CREATE Customer\_TimePeriod

Custom Action: copy

The behavior definition allows defining custom actions.

We defined a copy action for the Customer entity.

Why use it?

Efficiency: Quickly create similar records

Consistency: Maintain default values.

User Experience: Convenient for repetitive data entry.

Implementing the copy Action: Add a method in the local handler class to implement the copy logic.

define behavior for ZC_TMF_Customer alias Customer
authorization master ( instance )
draft table ZZTMFCUSTOMER_d
etag master last_changed_at
lock master total etag last_changed_at
early numbering
{
  // Root entity
  create;
  update;
  delete;
  action copy;

Implement the Method logic:

METHOD copy.


  DATA: lv_new_id TYPE zztmfcustomer-id,
        ls_customer TYPE zztmfcustomer,
        lt_accountref TYPE TABLE OF zztmfaccountref,
        lt_agreementre TYPE TABLE OF zztmfagreementre,
        lt_contactmedium TYPE TABLE OF zztmfcontactme,
        lt_creditprofile TYPE TABLE OF zztmfcreditpro,
        lt_eventinput TYPE TABLE OF zztmfeventinput,
        lt_eventsubscr TYPE TABLE OF zztmfeventsubscr,
        lt_characteris TYPE TABLE OF zztmfcharacteris,
        lt_paymentme TYPE TABLE OF zztmfpaymentme,
        lt_relatedpart TYPE TABLE OF zztmfrelatedpart,
        lt_timeperiod TYPE TABLE OF zztmftimeperiod.

  LOOP AT entities ASSIGNING FIELD-SYMBOL(<ls_entity>).

    " 1. Read original customer
    SELECT SINGLE * FROM zztmfcustomer  WHERE id = @<ls_entity>-customer_id INTO _customer.
    IF sy-subrc <> 0.
      CONTINUE.
    ENDIF.

    " 2. Get new customer id
    SELECT MAX( id ) FROM zztmfcustomer INTO _new_id.
    lv_new_id =  lv_new_id + 1.

    " 3. Insert new customer (copy, but new id)
    ls_customer-id = lv_new_id.
    INSERT zztmfcustomer FROM _customer.

    " 4. Copy AccountRef
    SELECT * FROM zztmfaccountref  WHERE id = @<ls_entity>-customer_id INTO TABLE _accountref.
    LOOP AT lt_accountref ASSIGNING FIELD-SYMBOL(<ls_acc>).
      <ls_acc>-id = lv_new_id.
    ENDLOOP.
    IF lt_accountref IS NOT INITIAL.
      INSERT zztmfaccountref FROM TABLE _accountref.
    ENDIF.

    " 5. Copy AgreementRe
    SELECT * FROM zztmfagreementre  WHERE id = @<ls_entity>-customer_id INTO TABLE _agreementre.
    LOOP AT lt_agreementre ASSIGNING FIELD-SYMBOL(<ls_agr>).
      <ls_agr>-id = lv_new_id.
    ENDLOOP.
    IF lt_agreementre IS NOT INITIAL.
      INSERT zztmfagreementre FROM TABLE _agreementre.
    ENDIF.

    " 6. Copy ContactMedium
    SELECT * FROM zztmfcontactme  WHERE id = @<ls_entity>-customer_id INTO TABLE _contactmedium.
    LOOP AT lt_contactmedium ASSIGNING FIELD-SYMBOL(<ls_con>).
      <ls_con>-id = lv_new_id.
    ENDLOOP.
    IF lt_contactmedium IS NOT INITIAL.
      INSERT zztmfcontactme FROM TABLE _contactmedium.
    ENDIF.

    " 7. Copy CreditProfile
    SELECT * FROM zztmfcreditpro  WHERE id = @<ls_entity>-customer_id INTO TABLE _creditprofile.
    LOOP AT lt_creditprofile ASSIGNING FIELD-SYMBOL(<ls_cred>).
      <ls_cred>-id = lv_new_id.
    ENDLOOP.
    IF lt_creditprofile IS NOT INITIAL.
      INSERT zztmfcreditpro FROM TABLE _creditprofile.
    ENDIF.

    " 8. Copy EventInput
    SELECT * FROM zztmfeventinput  WHERE id = @<ls_entity>-customer_id INTO TABLE _eventinput.
    LOOP AT lt_eventinput ASSIGNING FIELD-SYMBOL(<ls_evi>).
      <ls_evi>-id = lv_new_id.
    ENDLOOP.
    IF lt_eventinput IS NOT INITIAL.
      INSERT zztmfeventinput FROM TABLE _eventinput.
    ENDIF.

    " 9. Copy EventSubscription
    SELECT * FROM zztmfeventsubscr  WHERE id = @<ls_entity>-customer_id INTO TABLE _eventsubscr.
    LOOP AT lt_eventsubscr ASSIGNING FIELD-SYMBOL(<ls_evs>).
      <ls_evs>-id = lv_new_id.
    ENDLOOP.
    IF lt_eventsubscr IS NOT INITIAL.
      INSERT zztmfeventsubscr FROM TABLE _eventsubscr.
    ENDIF.

    " 10. Copy Characteristic
    SELECT * FROM zztmfcharacteris  WHERE id = @<ls_entity>-customer_id INTO TABLE _characteris.
    LOOP AT lt_characteris ASSIGNING FIELD-SYMBOL(<ls_char>).
      <ls_char>-id = lv_new_id.
    ENDLOOP.
    IF lt_characteris IS NOT INITIAL.
      INSERT zztmfcharacteris FROM TABLE _characteris.
    ENDIF.

    " 11. Copy PaymentMethod
    SELECT * FROM zztmfpaymentme  WHERE id = @<ls_entity>-customer_id INTO TABLE _paymentme.
    LOOP AT lt_paymentme ASSIGNING FIELD-SYMBOL(<ls_pay>).
      <ls_pay>-id = lv_new_id.
    ENDLOOP.
    IF lt_paymentme IS NOT INITIAL.
      INSERT zztmfpaymentme FROM TABLE _paymentme.
    ENDIF.

    " 12. Copy RelatedParty
    SELECT * FROM zztmfrelatedpart  WHERE id = @<ls_entity>-customer_id INTO TABLE _relatedpart.
    LOOP AT lt_relatedpart ASSIGNING FIELD-SYMBOL(<ls_rel>).
      <ls_rel>-id = lv_new_id.
    ENDLOOP.
    IF lt_relatedpart IS NOT INITIAL.
      INSERT zztmfrelatedpart FROM TABLE _relatedpart.
    ENDIF.

    " 13. Copy TimePeriod
    SELECT * FROM zztmftimeperiod  WHERE id = @<ls_entity>-customer_id INTO TABLE _timeperiod.
    LOOP AT lt_timeperiod ASSIGNING FIELD-SYMBOL(<ls_tim>).
      <ls_tim>-id = lv_new_id.
    ENDLOOP.
    IF lt_timeperiod IS NOT INITIAL.
      INSERT zztmftimeperiod FROM TABLE _timeperiod.
    ENDIF.

    " 14. Return new key
    APPEND VALUE #( %tky = VALUE #( customer_id = lv_new_id ) ) TO mapped-customer.

  ENDLOOP.

Draft Functionality

Draft functionality enhances the user experience by allowing users to create or modify business objects without immediately saving the changes to the database. This is particularly useful for complex objects or when multiple steps are involved in creating or editing data.

Draft Lifecycle:

  • Edit: The user starts editing an existing object or creates a new one, and the data is saved to a draft.
  • Activate: The user confirms the changes, and the draft data is saved to the active database table.
  • Discard: The user cancels the changes, and the draft data is deleted.
  • Resume: The user returns to a previously started draft to continue editing.

  • Draft Implementation Examples:

    • Activate:

      • This method activates a draft, saving the changes to the active table.
      • It reads the draft data from the zztmfcustomer_d (draft table).
      • It copies the data to a structure (ls_customer).
      • It updates the zztmfcustomer (active table) with the data.
      • It deletes the draft record from zztmfcustomer_d.
      • It includes error handling for database operations.

      • Discard:

        • This method discards a draft, deleting the draft data.
        • It deletes the draft record from the zztmfcustomer_d table.
        • It includes error handling.
        • Draft functions is handled automatically in the RAP framework, all you need to is create a table like below and specify it in Behaviour Definition.
@EndUserText.label : 'Table to Store Customer details'
@AbapCatalog.enhancement.category : #NOT_EXTENSIBLE
@AbapCatalog.tableCategory : #TRANSPARENT
@AbapCatalog.deliveryClass : #A
@AbapCatalog.dataMaintenance : #RESTRICTED
define table zztmfcustomer_d {

  key customer_id    : char10 not null;
  link               : char100;
  name               : char20 not null;
  status             : char20;
  statusreason       : char30;
  validfor           : datab;
  last_changed_at    : timestampl;
  locallastchangedat : timestampl;
  "%admin"           : include sych_bdl_draft_admin_inc;

}
​

ATTACHMENT HANDLING in RAP

@EndUserText.label : 'Customer Attachment'
@AbapCatalog.enhancement.category : #NOT_EXTENSIBLE
@AbapCatalog.tableCategory : #TRANSPARENT
@AbapCatalog.deliveryClass : #A
@AbapCatalog.dataMaintenance : #RESTRICTED
define table zztmcustatt {

  key attach_id   : char10 not null;
  key customer_id : char10 not null;
  file_name       : char100 not null;
  file_type       : char20 not null;
  file_content    : abap.rawstring(999999);
  created_on      : timestampl;
  last_changed_at : timestampl;

}


METHOD create_Attachment.
    DATA: lt_attach TYPE TABLE OF zztmcustatt,
          lv_max_id TYPE zztmcustatt-attach_id.

*    " Determine next attachment id

*
    LOOP AT entities ASSIGNING FIELD-SYMBOL(<ls_entity>).
      DATA(lt_target) = <ls_entity>-%target.
        SELECT MAX( attach_id ) FROM zztmcustatt  where customer_id = @<ls_entity>-customer_id INTO _max_id.
    IF sy-subrc = 0.
      " Convert lv_max_id to a number, increment
      lv_max_id = lv_max_id + 1.
    ELSE.
      lv_max_id = '0000000001'. " initial value, formatted as needed
    ENDIF.
      LOOP AT lt_target ASSIGNING FIELD-SYMBOL(<ls_target>).
        APPEND VALUE #(
          attach_id    = lv_max_id
          customer_id  = <ls_entity>-customer_id   " Parent key from the Customer
          file_name    = <ls_target>-FileName
          file_type    = <ls_target>-FileType
          file_content = <ls_target>-Attachment
          created_on   = sy-datum && sy-uzeit   " or use GET TIME STAMP
        ) TO lt_attach.
        " Increment attachment key (format as needed)
        lv_max_id = lv_max_id + 1.
      ENDLOOP.
    ENDLOOP.

    IF lt_attach IS NOT INITIAL.
      INSERT zztmcustatt FROM TABLE _attach.
      " Map created attachments back
      LOOP AT lt_attach ASSIGNING FIELD-SYMBOL(<ls_att>).
        APPEND VALUE #( %tky = VALUE #( AttachId = <ls_att>-attach_id ) )
          TO mapped-Attachment.
      ENDLOOP.
    ENDIF.
  ENDMETHOD.

 Create a Service Definition and Service Binding to test the application using FIORI ELEMENTS.

  • Creation of New Record with Draft Functionality & Attachment


     

    • The Records is not Created but its in Draft Status

       

    • A new Attachment is uploaded and Created

       

       

       

Conclusion

In Part 2, we covered the implementation details of our Customer Management application. We explored the role of the local handler class in processing business logic, implemented draft functionality to enhance the user experience, and enabled attachment handling to manage files.

This two-part series provided a comprehensive guide to building a Customer Management application with unmanaged RAP. By understanding the concepts and examples presented here, you can create your own robust and user-friendly RAP applications. Remember to adapt the code and techniques to the specific requirements of your projects.

 

With this you can integrate your RAP application with Process Build Automation for Approval. A workflow handler class should be called in the determination of RAP to call the created Workflow. 

As of now, SAP BTP Trial has not provided integration part for free, it can be done only in Licensed mode.

entry.title: Choosing the Right Analytical Tool: A Comprehensive Guide for SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition
entry.content:
Selecting the right analytical tool within the SAP S/4HANA Cloud framework can be pivotal to addressing your business needs effectively. Embedded analytics within SAP S/4HANA Cloud offers a suite of tools to answer diverse business questions, foster data-driven decision-making, and streamline operations. Here, we’ll delve into the factors that should guide your choice in selecting the most suitable embedded analytical tool for your organization and requirements. 

  1. Tailored Support for Diverse Needs
  2. Overview on all available analytical tools inside SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition
    • Review Booklet
    • Multidimensional Analysis
    • Overview Page
    • Analytical List Page
    • Smart Business KPIs
    • Embedded SAP Analytics Cloud Story
    • SAP Analysis for Microsoft Excel
  3. Checklist
  4. Feature / Function / Capability focus
  5. Self-service learning

Tailored Support for Diverse Needs

The landscape of business analytics is vast, and having the correct tool set can significantly enhance business operations by:

  • Addressing specific business queries and needs.
  • Increasing user adoption and engagement with analytical tools.
  • Enabling business self-service for ongoing data insights into operations.

When navigating through SAP S/4HANA Cloud's embedded analytics offerings, consider the following factors to align with your business goals: 

  1. 🧠 Key User Availability & Expertise: Ensure that the users have the necessary skills and expertise to interact with the chosen tool.
  2. 🏢 Line of Business (LoB): Matching the tool to the specific needs of departments such as finance, sales, or procurement can lead to more insightful analysis.
  3. 📅 Report Type and Cadence: Determine whether you require mass reports, individual reports, or a mix; and establish the frequency of these reports (daily, weekly, monthly).
  4. 📊 Report Complexity: Evaluate the complexity regarding multiple KPIs and dimensions to choose a suitable tool.
  5. 🌐 Business Domain Coverage: Establish whether your needs involve one or multiple domains.

Furthermore, different personas use analytics to drive specific aspects of their roles, leveraging different types and levels of data to meet their unique objectives and contribute to the overall success of the company. This table outlines the personas, their roles related to analytics, and the recommended tools to support those functions on high level.

 

PersonaRole Description and ExamplesRecommended Product
Executive LeadershipHigh-level strategic decision-making, financial performance tracking, and overall business health assessment.Enterprise SAP Analytics Cloud or SAP Business Data Cloud 
Managers (e.g., Sales, Supply Chain)Sales performance tracking, pipeline management, identifying sales opportunities/inventory management, supplier performance, and logistics optimization.Embedded analytics in SAP S/4HANA Cloud
Financial AnalystsBudget analysis, investment performance, managing the company's liquidity, funding, capital management, organize and control receivables and collections, etc.Embedded analytics in SAP S/4HANA Cloud
Financial PlanningProvides overall financial planning and performance management insights.Enterprise SAP Analytics Cloud e.g. via Rapid FP&A for SAP S/4HANA 

Now, let's go deeper and let me provide you an overview on the analytical tools that SAP offers inside SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition. More information can also be found here.

back to top

Review Booklet

As this is one of the new kids on the blog. I'd like to start with the Review Booklet. In essence, Review Booklets provide pre-configured, user-friendly business pages designed to address common and pertinent business inquiries for your day-to-day tasks right out of the box. They merge application logic and direction with built-in multidimensional analytics, enhanced further by advanced AI capabilities as they evolve. SAP ships already multiple Review Booklets with out-of-the-box content. We also know that our content never hits 100% of your requirements, thus it is possible to adjust the content via the Review Booklet Designer. An image or video says more that thousand words when it comes to data visualization, so please take a look at this example of a Review Booklet:

Key benefits of Review Booklet:

  1. Consolidated Reporting: Gather multiple reports into a single booklet format, streamlining access and enhancing information organization for more efficient review.
  2. Predefined Layouts for Business Queries: Utilize ready-made layouts tailored to address specific business questions, providing immediate insights without extensive customization.
  3. Guided Analysis with Multidimensional Insights: Experience guided and flexible analysis modes enriched by contextual actions and generic pivoting, seamlessly combining application logic with integrated multidimensional insights to support thorough exploration and understanding

back to top

Multidimensional Analysis

The second 'new' kid is the Multidimensional Analysis report. Multidimensional Analysis offers a flexible and interactive way to delve into complex datasets, enabling users to uncover insights more intuitively. It begins with an analytical query in its default view, serving as a starting point for exploration. From there, users can pivot and drill down into the data, adding dimensions, measures, and structures to rows and columns to tailor the analysis to specific needs. This approach is particularly beneficial for advanced users, facilitating deep data exploration and empowering them to discover trends and patterns that drive informed decision-making. For more info, check out this blog. Multidimensional Analysis will replace the reports based on the 'old' technology of Web Dynpro Grid in the future, so better get yourself familiar with it.

Key benefits of Multidimensional Analysis reports:

  1. Flexible and Interactive Exploration: Enjoy adaptability through interactive pivoting and drill-down capabilities, allowing users to dynamically manipulate data for deeper insights.
  2. Customizable Data Views: Begin with a default view of an analytical query and progressively enhance your exploration by adding various dimensions, measures, and structures to both rows and columns for tailored analysis.
  3. Advanced Data Exploration: Cater to advanced users seeking comprehensive understanding, offering powerful tools for extensive data investigation and analysis.

Figure: shows a Multidimensional Analysis on Income Statement and how to add a calculation on the fly.

back to top

Overview Page

An overview page is a unique SAP Fiori app designed to enhance user experience by offering a streamlined structure. Each overview page is composed of predefined cards of varying types displayed on a canvas beneath a filter bar. Adjustments made in the filter bar apply universally to all cards within the app, ensuring cohesiveness in data presentation. Users can interact with the cards by clicking on them, allowing navigation to additional apps such as reports, KPI details, or transactional applications.

Key benefits of Overview Page:

  1. KPI Cards for Immediate Insight: Deliver a quick and comprehensive snapshot of a domain by showcasing key performance measures, enabling users to grasp the overall picture swiftly.
  2. Aggregated Data Display: Present data in a consolidated format, streamlining information to enhance clarity and facilitate informed decision-making.
  3. Navigation Options: Enable deeper investigation through seamless navigation, positioning overview pages as an ideal entry point for comprehensive analysis.

Figure: Procurement Overview Page for Purchaser.

back to top

Analytical List Page

The Analytical List Page (ALP) is an integral component of SAP Fiori designed to transform business key performance indicators into dynamic analytical dashboards. These dashboards incorporate KPIs, charts, tables, and visual filters to provide insightful data visualizations. Powered by SAP UI annotations, ALP offers the flexibility to explore data from various angles, allowing for in-depth analysis of root causes and supporting timely decision-making. 

Key Benefits of ALP:

  1. Insight to Action is Key: Empower business users to process their work lists in context with their analytical findings, driving effective outcomes.
  2. Focus on Critical Issues: Easily highlight and address the most pressing concerns, and take immediate action without having to switch screens.
  3. Prioritize Efficiency: Sort and filter work lists to ensure that the most critical issues are prioritized and dealt with promptly.

Figure: Supervise Collection Worklist for Accounts Receivable Manager.

back to top

Smart Business KPIs (including Smart Business Runtime aka generic drill-down)

The KPIs are presented on tile surfaces using either simple numerical values or mini-charts, organized through the SAP Fiori Launchpad. Business users have the flexibility to select which KPIs to display on their personalized Launchpads, choosing from catalogs assigned to their roles. For deeper analysis, users can easily click on a tile to access detailed drill-down information. The Smart Business runtime environment provides access to detailed configurations of evaluations, enabling users to drill down and analyze KPIs in detail. The runtime is enhanced with smart controls, such as smart filters and smart charts, to further streamline the analytical process.

Key benefits of Smart Business KPI reports:

  1. Visual and Color-Coded KPI Representation: Display KPIs the SAP Fiori launchpad with simple numerical values, utilizing colors to offer immediate visual cues and enhance comprehension of performance levels.
  2. Instant Performance Overview: Provide an at-a-glance view of business performance, enabling quick assessments and timely decision-making.

Figure: Analysis section on the Fiori Launchpad for Accounts Receivables Managers opening the Smart Business generic drill-down app Overdue Receivables.

back to top

Embedded SAP Analytics Cloud Story

The SAP Analytics Cloud embedded version integrates seamlessly with the SAP Fiori UI of SAP S/4HANA Cloud and the so called Stories offer thereby an additional tool to analyze business data. Stories are designed to transform raw data into insightful visualizations, empowering users to enhance business performance and make informed, data-driven decisions including seamless navigation to and from other S/4HANA Cloud apps. These stories reveal hidden insights in your data by allowing the addition of text, images, and generating dynamic visualizations such as charts and graphs. Of course, SAP offers a large set of out-of-the-box content which can serve as a quick start and can be customized according to the requirements. 

Key benefits of an embedded SAP Analytics Cloud Story:

  1. Powerful Visualization and Feature-Rich Dashboards: Create dynamic and visually compelling analytical dashboards that enhance understanding and decision-making.
  2. Interactive Data Exploration: Easily explore data through linked analysis, page filters, chart filters, and input controls, providing a user-friendly experience for in-depth insights.
  3. Advanced Data Analysis: Utilize location analytics, perform calculations on the fly, and analyze multiple queries for comprehensive analysis.

Figure: Purchasing Spend Dashboard for Purchasers.

back to top

SAP Analysis for Microsoft Excel

There is also an option to analyze business data from SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition using SAP Analysis for Microsoft Excel. However, this reporting tool is not embedded within SAP S/4HANA Cloud, but can be opened via an app from SAP Fiori Launchpad. 

Figure: Opening SAP Analysis for Microsoft Excel from Fiori Launchpad. 

back to top

Checklist

Now consolidating all the information above, I come to the following checklists that shall help preselecting possible tool candidates by ticking the checkboxes according to your needs:

back to top

Feature / Function / Capabilities focus

Now that I provided an holistic overview on all the available options to analyze your business data, I'd like to get into the details. Important to know is that the following tables show the strengths of each tool, there is no one fits all solution as different information requirements and perspectives require different capabilities. 

I will explain the one or the other capability to make sure that you understand these tables correctly. Please leave a comment if something is not clear.

Legend 

++

Yes, supported and additional features/functions available

+

Yes, supported but limited (explanation in brackets)

-

Not supported

 

Visualization Capabilities

 

 

Analytical List Page

Embedded SAP Analytics Cloud Stories*

Smart Business KPI

Multidimensional Analysis

Review Booklet

Table

++

++

++

++

Variance / Difference support

+ (if variance/difference is a measure itself it can be added as second measure)

++

+ (needs to be enabled in underlying data source)

+ (user can add a calculated measure)

  • Single Values/Numeric Point: Simple KPI, e.g. sales volume, number of returns
  • Variance /Difference support:  a measure’s difference between time periods, e.g. difference of sales volume year over year (total or %)

Business User Capabilities (at runtime)

 

 

Analytical List Page

  • Ranking: filter by measure and select N top/bottom, e.g. top 5 customers by sales volume
  • Table freeze: freeze col and/or row for scrolling, defined by business user
  • Insight to action workflow: within one view analytical and transactional data, e.g. find critical tasks that need action and act upon these

Functional Capabilities

 

 

Analytical List Page

Multiple Pages

Saving personalized views: Support of Bookmarks / Variant management

++

++

  • Zero suppression: determine whether rows or columns that contain zeros as values are displayed or not displayed
  • Calculation on the fly: business or key user can define a calculation on top of the measures that the underlying data source provides, e.g. A-B, or more sophisticated like a restricted measure (e.g. sales volume of all customers who bought a specific material)
    • Attention: Best practice to add own calculations via the Custom Analytical Queries & Services app
  • Commenting: business user can add a comment on a specific value (i.e. cell-based)
  • Leverage generative AI: predefined, ready to use Business Insights. Analyze and summarize data set, calculate and explain context relevant financial KPIs (e.g. Cost Center Review Booklet)
  • Multiple Pages: not about views that can be changed but real pages that group different domains, views next to each other, e.g. like in a book with chapters

Data Capabilities

  • Ad-hoc reporting: non-periodic to answer ad-hoc questions, either in a pre-defined format or generated on demand, i.e. slice and dice / pivot table like
  • Data Write Back: if end user sees a record in the data that needs action, the user can navigate directly to the object page application and modify the record. E.g. ‘Supervise Collections Worklist‘ app: Assign or remove collection supervisors to/from collection items.

*for stories based on Optimized Story Experience

entry.title: Configuration the freight in the outbound delivery
entry.content:
Configuration:

1. define the condition type:

2. assign the condition type to price procedure

3. assign the condition type to DN price procedure

4. determine the DN price procedure

5. change the copy control setting (DN TO Billing)

Steps:

1. VA01 create the SO:

2. VL01N create the DN

3. VF01 create the billing document

entry.title: SAP Sapphire 2025 Is Where Quote-to-Cash Gets Exciting — Your Guide to the Top Sessions
entry.content:
In today’s world of subscriptions, usage-based pricing, and instant everything, Q2C is the engine that powers seamless buying experiences, recurring revenue, and future-proof finance operations. It’s where AI meets automation. Where billing meets experience. And where market leaders — from industrial giants to autonomous mobility innovators — are rewriting the rules.

At SAP Sapphire 2025, Q2C is taking the spotlight in Orlando and Madrid. We’re bringing together trailblazing customers, the latest SAP innovations, and some seriously cool tech — think generative AI, smart agents, and lightning-fast payment flows.

If you’re thinking, “this isn’t your typical billing session,” you’re right. Here’s what’s in store:

Top Quote-to-Cash Sessions at SAP Sapphire Orlando

🚀 Road Map Review

  • Harness growth & transformation with AI & insights in Opportunity to Cash: FIM2253

We’re putting real AI into real business. See how embedded intelligence and predictive analytics are helping companies get faster, leaner, and more future-proof in how they sell, bill, and collect.

🌟 Customer Success Sessions

  • Lessons learned implementation of SAP S/4HANA Cloud at Waymo: IND3890

What do self-driving cars have to do with billing? Everything. Waymo, the autonomous ride-hailing pioneer, is transforming how they run their entire order-to-cash process — using SAP S/4HANA Cloud to scale smarter, move faster, and handle the complexity of a future where software drives the business. Hear how they approached implementation, what they learned, and how automation plays a role in supporting their cutting-edge business model.

 

  • Streamlining software subscriptions for industrial software at Honeywell: IND2461

Even industrial companies are getting into subscription mode. Honeywell reimagined how it sells and manages industrial software by optimizing billing with BRIM.

 

  • Pfizer’s order-to-cash digital transformation: BAI2379

See how Pfizer transformed its O2C process on a global scale — improving compliance, order automation, and transparency in an industry where every transaction counts.

 

  • Nielsen's billing and revenue transformation: PAR1104

Join us to gain insights into Nielsen's journey towards operational excellence through intelligent automation and improved financial processes.

🧠 Strategy Talks

  • SAP goes SaaS: Transforming quote-to-cash in high-tech: DIS2508

High-tech companies are leading the charge into the SaaS world — and they need Q2C to keep up. Explore how usage-based pricing, scalable billing, and flexible monetization models are shaping the future.

 

  • Drive your service-centric growth with SAP Business Suite: ERP3331

The product-only era is over. Learn how companies are flipping the script and delivering outcomes, not just goods — and how SAP supports that shift end-to-end.

 

  • Manage subscriptions with SAP Billing and Revenue Innovation Management: FIM2722

Learn how Google powers its subscription business and quote-to-cash process with SAP BRIM and Revenue Accounting — including insights on system design, scalable architectures, and running SAP on Google Cloud.

 

  • SAP solutions for quote-to-cash management: Get onboard: FIM2255

New to Q2C or expanding your vision? This is your onboarding zone. Get a practical view of how SAP’s portfolio works together to deliver end-to-end revenue management.

 

  • How Joule Agents transform the quote-to-service process: CXP1670

Generative AI isn’t just a buzzword. See Joule — SAP’s AI-powered agent — in action as it automates decisions and drives outcomes across the entire Q2C flow.

🧪 Demo Pod That’ll Blow Your Mind

  • Quote to Cash for Business Innovation: FIM2568

Get hands-on with real use cases — from subscription billing to smart collections — and see how innovation drives personalization, speed, and revenue.

 

  • Next-generation, AI-powered cash visibility and instant payments: FIM2198

Real-time cash insights, automated reconciliation, and instant payments — this is next-level finance made real.

👔Ask the Expert

  • Selling and servicing configurable products and solutions to grow revenue: IND3257

Dive into advanced configuration, pricing, and service models — key to unlocking long-tail revenue in complex product businesses.

Q2C at Sapphire Madrid: Focused and Future-Ready

If you're attending in Madrid, here’s what to look out for:

🗺️ Roadmap Review

  • Subscription management and high-volume invoicing: FIM1436

See what’s next in BRIM for global businesses dealing with millions of transactions and multiple subscription models.

🌟 Customer Success Sessions

  • Criteo's successful billing transformation: PAR1337

Ad-tech leader Criteo revamped its billing model to move fast, scale globally, and stay agile with SAP BRIM. Get the behind-the-scenes story.

 🧪 Demo Pod

  • Quote to cash: FIM1327

Dive into our latest innovations across sales, billing, collections, and revenue management — all in one unified demo experience.

Join the Conversation

The world of monetization is changing. Whether you're launching a subscription product, scaling globally, or embedding AI into every process — quote-to-cash is where it all comes together.

📣Follow the conversation on social media #SAPSapphire 
📝Get an overview of the top finance sessions in my recent blog post covering the SAP Sapphire Financial Management Track
🔍Explore the full SAP Sapphire 2025 Session Catalog

entry.title: Streamlining 3SL Post Conversion Steps: Introducing CIAS and TDC Services
entry.content:
As the SAP's enterprise cloud software landscape continuously evolves, it is of paramount importance to harmonize the landscape for all the customers. 3-System landscape conversion program is thus one of SAP’s strategic programs.

Understanding the 3SL/CBC Conversion Program

The 3-system landscape (3SL) architecture provisions three systems—Development (D), Test (T), and Production (P)—to customers. This separation facilitates advanced development projects and enables developer extensibility options, leveraging SAP Central Business Configuration (CBC) to configure business processes within SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public edition. CBC’s objective is to allow the customer to implement end-to-end business processes across the intelligent enterprise from a central place.

Historically, SAP S/4HANA Cloud installations were based on a 2-system landscape consisting of a Quality and a Production system. Some of these SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public edition customers utilized the solution builder framework via the “Manage Your Solution” app while others have access to CBC to configure their business processes. The 3SL/CBC Conversion program aims to transition all 2SL customers to 3SL/CBC.

Post Conversion Challenge

Upon conversion, customers must take a few steps to ensure their new test system resembles their current quality system. Two critical steps include:

  • Populating the system with master data and test data for testing
  • Shifting the integration setup from the development system to the test system

These steps traditionally involve tedious manual copying of communication arrangements, systems, and users. Additionally, the new test system provided to customers lacks master and transactional data which to some customer offers a chance to load clean master and transaction test data but to the others involves manual steps to migrate data from their development system.

Latest Innovations

To address these challenges, SAP has developed two key services: the Cloud Integration Automation Service (CIAS) and the Test Data Conversion (TDC) Service.

Cloud Integration Automation Service (CIAS)

The CIAS re-wiring procedure guides customers through a workflow-like process to select source and target systems. It then automatically copies communication arrangements, systems, and users, eliminating much of the manual work. This automation not only saves time but also reduces the risk of errors, ensuring a smooth transition to the new system.

 

Test Data Conversion (TDC) Service

The TDC service ensures business continuity by transferring historical test data from the development system's customizing tenant to the new test system. This includes test automates, number ranges, multi-bank connectivity, and output management settings. By automating the transfer of master and test transactional data, TDC enables seamless testing in the new environment, allowing customers to continue their operations without interruption.

 

 

In summary, the Cloud Integration Automation Service and the Test Data Conversion Service streamlines processes, reduces manual workload post conversion to 3SL. 

Thank you Calin Cernea for your contributions and leading the workstream to enable these innovations.
